name: BFS Flood Fill (queue)
summary: Same fill, explored breadth-first: a queue serves pixels in the order they were discovered, so the recolored region grows outward one ring at a time.
visualizationIntent: init: draw the image grid, read the start pixel's original color, empty state overlay, empty queue. check: if the start pixel already equals the new color, stop early. update: recolor the start pixel and each matching neighbor to the new color, lighting them frontier as they are discovered and visited once served. move: dequeue the oldest pixel (current), examine its 4 neighbors, enqueue any that still share the original color. return: show the fully recolored region.

lineExplanations.3: Record how many rows the image has.
lineExplanations.4: Record how many columns each row has.
lineExplanations.5: Remember the start pixel's original color — the color we'll replace.
lineExplanations.6: Start an empty 'state' tracker (0 = unseen, 1 = waiting in the queue, 2 = done).
lineExplanations.7: Loop over each row to give it a matching tracker row.
lineExplanations.8: Add a fresh row of zeros so every pixel starts unseen.
lineExplanations.9: Placeholder for the current pixel's row; -1 means 'none yet'.
lineExplanations.10: Placeholder for the current pixel's column.
lineExplanations.11: The four orthogonal moves — up, down, right, left.
lineExplanations.12: If the start pixel is already the target color, there's nothing to do.
lineExplanations.13: Return the image unchanged to avoid an endless refill.
lineExplanations.14: Create an empty queue for the pixels to recolor.
lineExplanations.15: Enqueue the start pixel — the seed of the fill.
lineExplanations.16: Mark the start pixel as discovered.
lineExplanations.17: Recolor the start pixel to the new color.
lineExplanations.18: Keep filling while the queue has pixels waiting.
lineExplanations.19: Take the pixel from the FRONT of the queue — the oldest one discovered.
lineExplanations.22: Mark this pixel fully served — its neighbors are about to be checked.
lineExplanations.23: Try each of the four directions from this pixel.
lineExplanations.26: Is the neighbor in-bounds and still the original color?
lineExplanations.27: Mark that neighbor discovered so it is never queued twice.
lineExplanations.28: Recolor the neighbor to the new color.
lineExplanations.29: Add the neighbor to the BACK of the queue to spread the fill.
lineExplanations.30: The region is fully recolored — return the modified image.

syntaxExplanations.1: class Solution: is the class wrapper LeetCode expects.
syntaxExplanations.2: def floodFill(self, image, sr, sc, color): defines the method; image is the 2D grid, (sr,sc) the start pixel, color the new color.
syntaxExplanations.4: image[0] is the first row; len(...) gives the column count.
syntaxExplanations.5: image[sr][sc] reads the start pixel's current color.
syntaxExplanations.6: [] creates an empty list to fill with tracker rows.
syntaxExplanations.7: for r in range(rows): loops r over each row index.
syntaxExplanations.8: [0] * cols builds a row of that many zeros; .append adds it.
syntaxExplanations.9: cur_r = -1 sets a sentinel; -1 is not a valid index.
syntaxExplanations.10: cur_c = -1 does the same for the column.
syntaxExplanations.11: A list of [row-delta, col-delta] pairs for the 4 directions.
syntaxExplanations.12: start_color == color tests whether a fill would change anything.
syntaxExplanations.13: return image exits early with the image untouched.
syntaxExplanations.14: [] makes an empty list used as a queue.
syntaxExplanations.15: append([sr, sc]) adds the start pixel's [row, col] to the back.
syntaxExplanations.17: image[sr][sc] = color paints the start pixel.
syntaxExplanations.18: while len(queue) > 0: runs while the queue isn't empty.
syntaxExplanations.19: queue.pop(0) removes and returns the FRONT item — index 0, not the end. That single 0 is the only difference from DFS.
syntaxExplanations.20: cell[0] reads the pair's first element (row).
syntaxExplanations.22: state[cur_r][cur_c] = 2 marks the pixel fully processed.
syntaxExplanations.23: for d in dirs: loops over each direction.
syntaxExplanations.24: cur_r + d[0] gives the neighbor's row.
syntaxExplanations.25: cur_c + d[1] gives the neighbor's column.
syntaxExplanations.26: Chained 'and' checks bounds and that the neighbor matches start_color.
syntaxExplanations.29: append([nr, nc]) puts the neighbor at the back of the queue.
syntaxExplanations.30: return image hands back the recolored grid.
